zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Robert Bacon於1298年出生在英國諾福爾可巴根斯索普時，他的父親Baron John Bacon I26歲，母親Alice Assey19歲。他與Mary在英國英格蘭素福爾可貝汀菲兒結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1328年在英國素福爾可布拉德費爾德 聖 喬治享年30歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Robert Bacon于1298年出生在英国諾福爾可巴根斯索普时，他的父亲Baron John Bacon I26岁，母亲Alice Assey19岁。他与Mary在英国英格兰素福爾可貝汀菲兒结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1328年在英国素福爾可布拉德費爾德 聖 喬治享年30岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bacon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Norman French personal name Bacun, derived from the ancient Germanic name Bac(c)o, Bahho, based on the element bag ‘(to) fight, (to) dispute’. The name was relatively common among the Normans in the form Bacus, of which the oblique case was Bacon.

English and French: from Middle English, Old French bacun, bacon ‘bacon’ (a word of ancient Germanic origin, akin to Back 3), probably a metonymic occupational name for a preparer and seller of cured pork.

History: Gilles Bacon from Normandy, France, is documented in Quebec City, QC, in 1647. — Michael Bacon from England arrived in Dedham, MA, in 1640. Nathanial Bacon, from Stratton, Cornwall, arrived in Barnstaple, MA, in 1639. Another Nathaniel Bacon (1647–76), from Friston Hall, Suffolk, emigrated to VA and settled at Curl's Neck on the James river.

variants.name: Bagg
meanings: from Middle English bagge ‘bag, pack, bundle’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of bags and sacks of various kinds, including wallets and purses. It might also have been used for a beggar, or for one who carried their belongings in a bag.
from the ancient Germanic personal name Bago (Bac(c)o, Bahho), from the root bag- ‘to fight’ (see Bacon 2).

variants.name: Back
meanings: English (Devon, Kent, Sussex, and Norfolk): from the Middle English personal name Bakke (Old English Bacca). It is of uncertain origin, but may have been a byname in the same sense as 3.
English: nickname from Middle English bakke ‘bat’, of uncertain application, perhaps a nickname for a person with poor eyesight, from the expression ‘blind as a bat’.

English: from Middle English bakke ‘back’ (Old English bæc), hence a nickname for someone with a hunched back or some other noticeable peculiarity of the back or spine, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or ridge, or at the rear of a settlement.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Robert Bacon was born in 1298, in Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England, his father, Baron John Bacon I, was 26 and his mother, Alice Assey, was 19. He married Mary in Bedingfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1328, in Bradfield St George, Suffolk, England, at the age of 30.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Robert Bacon est né en 1298, à Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Baron John Bacon I, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Alice Assey, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Mary à Bedingfield, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1328, à Bradfield St George, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 30 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Robert Bacon 1298 in Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Baron John Bacon I 26 und seine Mutter Alice Assey 19. Er hat in Bedingfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Mary geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1328 im Alter von 30 Jahren in Bradfield St George, Suffolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Robert Bacon è nato il nel 1298 nel Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Baron John Bacon I, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Alice Assey, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Mary nel Bedingfield, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1328, nel Bradfield St George, Suffolk, England, all'età di 30 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Robert Baconが1298年にイギリスノーフォルクバコンスソルペで生まれた時，父Baron John Bacon Iは26歳，母Alice Asseyは19歳であった。イギリスイギリススフォルクベディングフィールドでMaryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1328年にイギリススフォルクブラドフィールド スト ジョーッジで30歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Robert Bacon(이)가 1298년 영국 노르폴크 바콘스소에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Baron John Bacon I(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Assey(은)는 19세였다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 베딩필드에서 Mary(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1328년 영국 수폴크 브래드필드 스트 조지에서 30세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele casou-se com Mary em Bedingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1328, em Bradfield St George, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 30 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Robert Bacon родился в 1298 г. в местности бакунстаурп, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Baron John Bacon I было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Assey, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary в местности бедингфиелд, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1328 г. в местности брадфиелд ст джеордж, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 30 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Robert Bacon nació en 1298, en Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Baron John Bacon I, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Alice Assey, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Mary en Bedingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1328, en Bradfield St George, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 30 años.
